primary_sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
primary_sources.relevant_rule: If-Match lets a client make a state-changing request conditional on the current representation matching an observed validator, preventing the lost-update problem.

primary_sources.title: GitHub REST API: repository contents
primary_sources.relevant_rule: Updating an existing repository file requires its current blob SHA; conflicting parallel updates must be serialized or retried.

finding: Shared-state writers should use optimistic concurrency: capture the exact blob SHA they observed, reject a write when the current SHA differs, and refetch/merge/retry rather than force-overwrite. Ownership remains a separate authorization check.
design_implication: Automation teams can safely update owned shared records only when both the observed SHA still matches and ownership permits replacement. Append-only evidence records remain preferred because they reduce collision scope.

verification.method: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical source before commit, followed by committed-source fetch inspection
cases: matching SHA plus append-only operation allowed
stale foreign-owned replacement reports both failures

uncertainty: The guard models repository-level write safety but is not yet wired into every automation writer or GitHub workflow.
falsification_route: Demonstrate a stale write accepted by the module.
Demonstrate a non-owner replacement accepted by the module.
Wire the guard into a real shared-state update and show that GitHub still accepts a conflicting stale SHA without an explicit force path.
limits: A matching SHA prevents a known stale write but does not prove semantic correctness.
Append-only records can still contain false or low-quality claims.
A force branch update can bypass the protocol and must remain prohibited.
privacy_boundary: Contains only public standards, repository paths, synthetic SHA fixtures, and commit identifiers; no private chat, health, credentials, or personal data.
